  • README: Suggest user.email to be 41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1707

v4.1.4

  • Disable extensions.worktreeConfig when disabling sparse-checkout by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1692
  • Add dependabot config by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1688
  • Bump the minor-actions-dependencies group with 2 updates by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1693
  • Bump word-wrap from 1.2.3 to 1.2.5 by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1643

v4.1.3

  • Check git version before attempting to disable sparse-checkout by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1656
  • Add SSH user parameter by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1685
  • Update actions/checkout version in update-main-version.yml by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1650

v4.1.2

  • Fix: Disable sparse checkout whenever sparse-checkout option is not present @​dscho in actions/checkout#1598

v4.1.1

  • Correct link to GitHub Docs by @​peterbe in actions/checkout#1511
  • Link to release page from what's new section by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1514

v4.1.0

  • Add support for partial checkout filters

Changelog

v4.2.2

  • url-helper.ts now leverages well-known environment variables by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1941
  • Expand unit test coverage for isGhes by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1946

v4.2.1

  • Check out other refs/* by commit if provided, fall back to ref by @​orhantoy in actions/checkout#1924

v4.2.0

  • Add Ref and Commit outputs by @​lucacome in actions/checkout#1180
  • Dependency updates by @​dependabot- actions/checkout#1777, actions/checkout#1872

v4.1.7

  • Bump the minor-npm-dependencies group across 1 directory with 4 updates by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1739
  • Bump actions/checkout from 3 to 4 by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1697
  • Check out other refs/* by commit by @​orhantoy in actions/checkout#1774
  • Pin actions/checkout's own workflows to a known, good, stable version. by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1776

v4.1.6

  • Check platform to set archive extension appropriately by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1732

v4.1.5

  • Update NPM dependencies by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1703
  • Bump github/codeql-action from 2 to 3 by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1694
  • Bump actions/setup-node from 1 to 4 by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1696

  • README: Suggest user.email to be 41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1707

v4.1.4

  • Disable extensions.worktreeConfig when disabling sparse-checkout by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1692
  • Add dependabot config by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1688
  • Bump the minor-actions-dependencies group with 2 updates by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1693
  • Bump word-wrap from 1.2.3 to 1.2.5 by @​dependabot in actions/checkout#1643

v4.1.3

  • Check git version before attempting to disable sparse-checkout by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1656
  • Add SSH user parameter by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1685
  • Update actions/checkout version in update-main-version.yml by @​jww3 in actions/checkout#1650

v4.1.2

  • Fix: Disable sparse checkout whenever sparse-checkout option is not present @​dscho in actions/checkout#1598

v4.1.1

  • Correct link to GitHub Docs by @​peterbe in actions/checkout#1511
  • Link to release page from what's new section by @​cory-miller in actions/checkout#1514

v4.1.0

  • Add support for partial checkout filters

db","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-02T02:43:00Z","updated_at":"2024-11-02T02:43:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I've been looking into why `juliaup update` hangs for a while before doing any substantial downloads, even on very fast connections.

I think the issue is that this file may be served via a CDN that needs its caching settings tweaked to be less lazy
https://julialang-s3.julialang.org/juliaup/RELEASECHANNELDBVERSION

It's a very small file, so doesn't make any sense that it takes 7s to download.. so I believe the time is the CDN refreshing a local cache.

--- 

From a profile during that quiet ~10s it can be seen that `update_version_db` is spending 7s waiting on `reqwest`.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af5baf36-ccb4-49b6-99a2-e1b9d5f43d7a)



cc. @giordano @davidanthoff

been looking into why `juliaup update` hangs for a while before doing any substantial downloads, even on very fast connections.

I think the issue is that this file may be served via a CDN that needs its caching settings tweaked to be less lazy
https://julialang-s3.julialang.org/juliaup/RELEASECHANNELDBVERSION

It's a very small file, so doesn't make any sense that it takes 7s to download.. so I believe the time is the CDN refreshing a local cache.

--- 

From a profile during that quiet ~10s it can be seen that `update_version_db` is spending 7s waiting on `reqwest`.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/af5baf36-ccb4-49b6-99a2-e1b9d5f43d7a)



cc. @giordano @davidanthoff

PRs:
- [x] #50832 <!-- Subtype: bug fix for bounds with deeper covariant var -->
- [x] #51782 <!-- Fix remove-addrspaces pass in the presence of globals with addrspaces -->
- [x] #55720 <!-- Fix `pkgdir` for extensions -->
- [x] #55773 <!-- Add compat entry for `Base.donotdelete` -->
- [x] #55886 <!-- irrationals: restrict assume effects annotations to known types -->
- [x] #55867 <!-- update `hash` doc string: `widen` not required any more -->
- [x] #56148 <!-- Make loading work when stdlib deps are missing in the manifest -->
- [x] #55870 <!-- fix infinite recursion in `promote_type` for `Irrational` -->
- [x] #56252 <!-- REPL: fix brace detection when ' is used for transpose -->
- [x] #56264 <!-- inference: fix inference error from constructing invalid `TypeVar` -->
- [x] #56276 <!-- move time_imports and trace_* macros to Base but remain owned by InteractiveUtils -->
- [x] #56254 <!-- REPL: don't complete str and cmd macros when the input matches the internal name like `r_` to `r"` -->
- [x] #56280 <!-- Fix trampoline warning on x86 as well -->
- [x] #56304 <!-- typeintersect: more fastpath to skip intersect under circular env -->
- [x] #56306 <!-- InteractiveUtils.jl: fixes issue where subtypes resolves bindings and causes deprecation warnings  -->
- [x] #42080 <!-- recommend explicit `using Foo: Foo, ...` in package code (was: "using considered harmful") -->
- [x] #55299 <!-- typeintersect: fix bounds merging during inner `intersect_all`. -->

Need manual backport:
- [ ] #55405 <!-- handle unbound vars in NTuple fields -->
- [ ] #55744 <!-- fix #45494, error in ssa conversion with complex type decl -->
- [ ] #55890 <!-- Profile: fix order of fields in heapsnapshot & improve formatting -->

Contains multiple commits, manual intervention needed:
- [ ] #54009 <!-- allow extensions to trigger from packages in [deps] -->
- [ ] #54303 <!-- LinearAlgebra: improve type-inference in Symmetric/Hermitian matmul -->
- [ ] #52694 <!-- Reinstate similar for AbstractQ for backward compatibility -->
- [ ] #54738 <!-- serialization: fix relocatability bug -->
- [ ] #55052 <!-- Fix `(l/r)mul!` with `Diagonal`/`Bidiagonal` -->
- [ ] #55251 <!-- Restrict binary ops for Diagonal and Symmetric to Number eltypes -->
- [ ] #55306 <!-- AllocOpt: Fix stack lowering where alloca continas boxed and unboxed data -->
- [ ] #55729 <!-- Avoid confounding compilation side effects of `@time_imports` -->
- [ ] #55589 <!-- prevent loading other extensions when precompiling an extension -->
- [ ] #55877 <!-- fix FileWatching designs and add workaround for a stat bug on Apple -->
- [ ] #56234 <!-- Revert "Extensions: make loading of extensions independent of what packages are in the sysimage (#52841) -->
- [ ] #56311 <!-- Fix `log_quasitriu` for internal scaling `s=0` -->
- [ ] #56369 <!-- cache the `find_all_in_cache_path` call during parallel precompilation -->

Non-merged PRs with backport label:
- [ ] #55958 <!-- also redirect JL_STDERR etc. when redirecting to devnull -->
- [ ] #55956 <!-- Make threadcall gc safe -->
- [ ] #55910 <!-- Prevent extensions from blocking parallel pre-compilation -->
- [ ] #55813 <!-- Check for conflicting `@ccallable` name before JIT registration -->
- [ ] #55534 <!-- Set stdlib sources as read-only during installation -->
- [ ] #55499 <!-- propagate the terminal's `displaysize` to the `IOContext` used by the REPL -->
- [ ] #55311 <!-- docs: Add headers for easier Core, Base and stdlib reference -->
- [ ] #55220 <!-- `isfile_casesensitive` fixes on Windows -->
- [ ] #55169 <!-- `propertynames` for SVD respects private argument -->
- [ ] #51479 <!-- prevent code loading from lookin in the versioned environment when building Julia -->
- [ ] #50813 <!-- More doctests for Sockets and capitalization fix -->
- [ ] #50157 <!-- improve docs for `@inbounds` and `Base.@propagate_inbounds` My understanding:
- stdlib - package that ships with julia (no git-tree-sha1 in the manifest)
- upgradable stdlib - package that ships with julia, which can optionally be upgraded (gets a git-tree-sha1 if upgraded)
- former stdlibs - a package that used to be shipped with julia but isn't any longer (no packages yet, AFAIK)

If a julia 2.0 is cut, then upgradable stdlibs are primed to become former stdlibs, i.e. not shipped with julia, but before that it's confusing I think.

Also I don't believe we document that some stdlibs can be Behaviour

When loading some packages in REPL, `REPLExt` throws an exception. 

### Expected behaviour

No exception thrown?

### Reproduce

To reproduce:
1. `julia-release --startup-file=no -t 1`
2. `using IJulia` (or other packages)

Packages that reproduce the bug:
- `IJulia`
- `Pluto`
- `CairoMakie`

Packages that don't reproduce the bug:
- `StaticArrays`
- `PackageCompiler`

### Version info:

 - `@profile foo()` to profile a specific call.
 - `Profile.print()` to print the report. Paths are clickable links in supported terminals and specialized for JULIA_EDITOR etc.
 - `Profile.clear()` to clear the buffer.
-- Send a $(Sys.isbsd() ? "SIGINFO (ctrl-t)" : "SIGUSR1") signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print.
+- Send a SIGUSR1 (on linux) or SIGINFO (on macOS) signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print. i.e. `kill -s SIGUSR1/SIGINFO $julia_pid`. ```suggestion
- Send a SIGUSR1 (on linux) or SIGINFO (on macOS) signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print. i.e. `kill -s SIGUSR1/SIGINFO 1234`, where 1234 is the pid of the julia process. On macOS & BSD platforms `ctrl-t` can be used directly.

 - `@profile foo()` to profile a specific call.
 - `Profile.print()` to print the report. Paths are clickable links in supported terminals and specialized for JULIA_EDITOR etc.
 - `Profile.clear()` to clear the buffer.
-- Send a $(Sys.isbsd() ? "SIGINFO (ctrl-t)" : "SIGUSR1") signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print.
+- Send a SIGUSR1 (on linux) or SIGINFO (on macOS) signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print. i.e. `kill -s SIGUSR1/SIGINFO 1234`, where 1234 is the pid of the julia process. On macOS & BSD platforms `ctrl-t` can be used directly.","path":"stdlib/Profile/src/Profile.jl","commit_id":"ee03e23e16a4c2052369b85e83e773b177a29c6e","original_commit_id":"ee03e23e16a4c2052369b85e83e773b177a29c6e","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion
- Send a SIGUSR1 (on linux) or SIGINFO (on macOS/BSD) signal to the process to automatically trigger a profile and print. i.e. `kill -s SIGUSR1/SIGINFO 1234`, where 1234 is the pid of the julia process. On macOS & BSD platforms `ctrl-t` can be used directly.

that `__init__` runs before `using` returns. Could be slightly breaking for people that do crazy things inside `__init__`, but just don't do that. Since extensions then probably load after `__init__` (or at least, run their `__init__` after), this is a partial step towards changing things so that extensions are guaranteed to load if using all of their triggers before the corresponding `using` returns

Fixes #55556

repeatedly","user":{"login":"lukas-weber","id":49278367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5Mjc4MzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49278367?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber","html_url":"https://github.com/lukas-weber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-11T19:32:22Z","updated_at":"2024-11-12T04:22:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I noticed that precompilation between parallel Julia processes, which worked really well since Julia 1.10, has slowed down massively with the release of Julia 1.11. The console output suggests that this is caused by repeated recompilations of packages that have already been precompiled by another process.

# Steps to reproduce
To reproduce this, we can load a package that takes long to precompile, such as MathOptInterface.

Create a file `test.jl` with the contents
```julia 
using MathOptInterface
```
and to make precompilation retrigger easily, we add `MathOptInterface` in development mode and subsequently edit one of its files.
```
] activate .
] dev MathOptInterface
```
To reduce noise, we also first run a global precompilation to make sure all dependencies are precompiled.

Given this preparation we can do the following:

```
julia +1.10 --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.precompile()'
echo "# modify" >> ~/.julia/dev/MathOptInterface/src/MathOptInterface.jl
time mpirun -n 5 julia +1.10 --project test.jl
```
which takes 62 secs on my machine. Now repeat for Julia 1.11.1

```
julia +1.11 --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.precompile()'
echo "# modify" >> ~/.julia/dev/MathOptInterface/src/MathOptInterface.jl
time mpirun -n 5 julia +1.11 --project test.jl
```
This takes 96 secs and now also produces the log
```
Precompiling project...
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 52 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
  52337.8 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 55 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  60610.2 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 64 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  70583.5 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 73 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  80622.7 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 84 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  90628.9 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 94 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
```
Seemingly there is a 10 second extra cost per MPI rank, which makes precompilation increasingly slower for large parallel runs with hundreds of processes.

The issue persists in the nightly PRs:
- [x] #55886 <!-- irrationals: restrict assume effects annotations to known types -->
- [x] #55867 <!-- update `hash` doc string: `widen` not required any more -->
- [x] #56084 <!-- slightly improve inference in precompilation code -->
- [x] #56088 <!-- make `Base.ANSIIterator` have a concrete field -->
- [x] #54093 <!-- Fix `JULIA_CPU_TARGET` being propagated to workers precompiling stdlib pkgimages -->
- [x] #56165 <!-- Fix markdown list in installation.md -->
- [x] #56148 <!-- Make loading work when stdlib deps are missing in the manifest -->
- [x] #56174 <!-- Fix implicit `convert(String, ...)` in several places -->
- [x] #56159 <!-- Add invalidation barriers for `displaysize` and `implicit_typeinfo` -->
- [x] #56089 <!-- Call `MulAddMul` instead of multiplication in _generic_matmatmul! -->
- [x] #56195 <!-- Include default user depot when JULIA_DEPOT_PATH has leading empty entry -->
- [x] #56215 <!-- [REPL] fix lock ordering mistake in load_pkg -->
- [x] #56251 <!-- REPL: run repl hint generation for modeswitch chars when not switching -->
- [x] #56092 <!-- stream: fix reading LibuvStream into array -->
- [x] #55870 <!-- fix infinite recursion in `promote_type` for `Irrational` -->
- [x] #56227 <!-- Do not call `rand` during sysimage precompilation -->
- [x] #55741 <!-- Change annotations to use a NamedTuple -->
- [x] #56149 <!-- Specialize adding/subtracting mixed Upper/LowerTriangular -->
- [x] #56214 <!-- fix precompile process flags -->
- [x] #54471
- [x] https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55622
- [x] https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55704
- [x] https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55764

Need manual backport:
- [ ] #55908 <!-- add logic to prefer loading modules that are already loaded -->
- [ ] #56097 <!-- rearrange jl_delete_thread to be thread-safe -->

Contains multiple commits, manual intervention needed:
- [ ] #55877 <!-- fix FileWatching designs and add workaround for a stat bug on Apple -->

Non-merged PRs with backport label:
- [ ] #56156 <!-- precompile: compile inlinable methods that were inferred -->
- [ ] #55958 <!-- also redirect JL_STDERR etc. when redirecting to devnull -->
- [ ] #55956 <!-- Make threadcall gc safe -->
- [ ] #55910 <!-- Prevent extensions from blocking parallel pre-compilation -->
- [ ] #55871 <!-- lowering: don't reverse handler order in `(pop-handler-list ...)` -->
- [ ] #55813 <!-- Check for conflicting `@ccallable` name before JIT registration -->

- [ ] #55458 <!-- Allow for generically extracting unannotated string -->
- [ ] #55457 <!-- Make AnnotateChar equality consider annotations -->
- [ ] #55220 <!-- `isfile_casesensitive` fixes on Windows -->
- [ ] #55169 <!-- `propertynames` for SVD respects private argument -->
- [ ] #54457 <!-- Make `String(::Memory)` copy -->
- [ ] #53957 <!-- tweak how filtering is done for what packages should be precompiled -->
- [ ] #51479 <!-- prevent code loading from lookin in the versioned environment when building Julia -->
- [ ] #50813 <!-- More doctests for Sockets and capitalization fix -->

tab completion freezes the REPL. For example, I type
```
julia> complicated_hard_to_infer_function(f(x) * y, g(x), TAB
```
and this gives me a frozen REPL for a few minutes, where not even Ctrl-C does something useful. Not a great user experience (in particular the Ctrl-C not responding part).

@MasonProtter provided the following reproducer (my original example is free of `@generated`):
```
julia> @generated function foo()
           @info "Compiling foo()"
           sleep(10)
           (;a=1, b=2)
       end;

julia> foo().[ Info: Compiling foo()
```

This is with julia 1.10.

Can we make the under the hood type inference give up if the result cannot be returned "immediately"? Alternatively, can we make Ctrl-C work?

Edit: It is really bad on 1.11, where this is triggered by the tab completion "hint", so almost always.

Edit edit: For the fellow victims of this: One can turn it off (on 1.11) via
```
Base.active_repl.options.hint_tab_completes = false
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":5,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2480699462","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434#issuecomment-2480699462","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434","id":2480699462,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T3HhG","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-16T18:14:11Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T18:14:11Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I'm putting this on the 1.12 milestone as I don't believe it's properly fixable on 1.11

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55878","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55878/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55878/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55878/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55878","id":2548677742,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6X6bxu","number":55878,"title":"InexactError: Int64(1.0) on macOS (CI)","user":{"login":"lmiq","id":31046348,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMxMDQ2MzQ4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/31046348?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq","html_url":"https://github.com/lmiq","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lmiq/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":338328723,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwzMzgzMjg3MjM=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/heisenbug","name":"heisenbug","color":"e13d21","default":false,"description":"This bug occurs unpredictably"},{"id":2251536036,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwyMjUxNTM2MDM2","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/rr%20trace%20wanted","name":"rr trace wanted","color":"b60205","default":false,"description":"An rr trace would help with debugging this issue - you can help out by creating one"},{"id":4387759152,"node_id":"LA_kwDOABkWpM8AAAABBYfkMA","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/compiler:effects","name":"compiler:effects","color":"2D3F35","default":false,"description":"effect analysis"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":25,"created_at":"2024-09-25T18:12:36Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T18:49:42Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I experienced this (non-reproducible) error in a CI run on the `pre` release (1.11 now), in the MacOS platform:

```julia
 ERROR: LoadError: TaskFailedException

    nested task error: InexactError: Int64(1.0)
```

The line where the error occurred was:

```julia
            index = floor(Int, xi) + 1
```

in which `xi` is a `Float64`. As far as I understand, the result of `floor` should always return an exactly representable Int, so the error should not occur. 

When reporting this on Zulip, it, @jlumpe (sorry if the tag is wrong), reported having observed two times similar errors:

https://github.com/mikeingold/MeshIntegrals.jl/actions/runs/10965929580/job/30452792024?pr=73#step:7:191
https://github.com/JoshuaLampert/DispersiveShallowWater.jl/actions/runs/10811172112/job/29990016675?pr=150#step:7:8511

But these with 1.10. 

Thus, although hard to reproduce, might be something worth investigating. tab completion freezes the REPL. For example, I type
```
julia> complicated_hard_to_infer_function(f(x) * y, g(x), TAB
```
and this gives me a frozen REPL for a few minutes, where not even Ctrl-C does something useful. Not a great user experience (in particular the Ctrl-C not responding part).

@MasonProtter provided the following reproducer (my original example is free of `@generated`):
```
julia> @generated function foo()
           @info "Compiling foo()"
           sleep(10)
           (;a=1, b=2)
       end;

julia> foo().[ Info: Compiling foo()
```

This is with julia 1.10.

Can we make the under the hood type inference give up if the result cannot be returned "immediately"? Alternatively, can we make Ctrl-C work?

Edit: It is really bad on 1.11, where this is triggered by the tab completion "hint", so almost always.

Edit edit: For the fellow victims of this: One can turn it off (on 1.11) via
```
Base.active_repl.options.hint_tab_completes = false
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434/reactions","total_count":5,"+1":5,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2480736029","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434#issuecomment-2480736029","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55434","id":2480736029,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6T3Qcd","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-16T18:54:21Z","updated_at":"2024-11-16T18:54:21Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"Are you following the recommended way to disable it? release-1.11"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-09-27T21:19:43Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T16:16:01Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/55910","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55910","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55910.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/55910.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"Previously our precompilation code was causing anything with package A as a dependency to wait on all of A's extensions and weakdeps to finish before starting to pre-compile, even if it can't actually load those weakdeps (or the extensions themselves)

This would lead to a pre-compile ordering like:
```
A        B
 \      / \
  Ext AB   \
     |     /
     C    /
      \  /
       D
```

Here `C` cannot pre-compile in parallel with `Ext {A,B}` and `B`, because it has to wait for `Ext {A,B}` to finish pre-compiling. That happens even though `C` has no way to load either of these.

This change updates the pre-compile ordering to be more parallel, reflecting the true place where `Ext {A,B}` can be loaded:
```
  A       B
 / \     / \
C   Ext AB  |
 \    |    /
  \-- D --/
```

which allows `C` to compile in parallel with `B` and `Ext{A,B}`

Please review 55b40ed91fe9f460179deb4b6a3794669e03fdd6 (that's the only commit w/ important default","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":542101692,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw1NDIxMDE2OTI=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Pkg.jl/labels/enhancement","name":"enhancement","color":"84b6eb","default":true,"description":""}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":5,"created_at":"2024-05-22T18:18:19Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T16:59:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Since `PRESERVE_TIERED_INSTALLED` was added to [Pkg.add](https://pkgdocs.julialang.org/v1/api/#Pkg.add) in 1.9 I've had it enabled as the default via the env var
```
JULIA_PKG_PRESERVE_TIERED_INSTALLED = true
```
and I've not seen any issues with getting stuck on older package versions, as was feared.

In short it tries harder to avoid updating packages that are already installed, to minimize installation and re-precompilation.

Given precompilation times have been increasing as precompilation workload coverage increases I think it would be good to make that the default behavior.

It'd be good to hear from others that have used that setting to see if there are any negatives identified.

┌ Warning: Circular dependency detected. Precompilation will be skipped for:

│   Base.PkgId(Base.UUID("244f68ed-b92b-5712-87ae-6c617c41e16a"), "NNlibAMDGPUExt")
│   Base.PkgId(Base.UUID("06b0261c-7a9b-5753-9bdf-fd6840237b4a"), "StaticArrayInterfaceStaticArraysExt")
│   Base.PkgId(Base.UUID("21141c5a-9bdb-4563-92ae-f87d6854732e"), "AMDGPU")
│   Base.PkgId(Base.UUID("9f7883ad-71c0-57eb-9f7f-b5c9e6d3789c"), "Tracker")
└ @ Base.Precompilation precompilation.jl:511
```
Now
```
julia> Pkg.precompile()
┌ Warning: Circular dependency detected. Precompilation will be skipped for:
│   CircularDep3
│   CircularDep2
│   CircularDep1
│   Foo → CircularExt
└ @ Base.Precompilation precompilation.jl:572
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56621/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56621/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2489296929","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56621#issuecomment-2489296929","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56621","id":2489296929,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6UX6gh","user":{"login":"IanButterworth","id":1694067,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE2OTQwNjc=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1694067?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth","html_url":"https://github.com/IanButterworth","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/IanButterworth/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-20T18:37:03Z","updated_at":"2024-11-20T18:37:03Z","author_association":"MEMBER","body":"I don't immediately know if these are ordered meaningfully. Behaviour

When loading some packages in REPL, `REPLExt` throws an exception. 

### Expected behaviour

No exception thrown?

### Reproduce

To reproduce:
1. `julia-release --startup-file=no -t 1`
2. `using IJulia` (or other packages)

Packages that reproduce the bug:
- `IJulia`
- `Pluto`
- `CairoMakie`

Packages that don't reproduce the bug:
- `StaticArrays`
- `PackageCompiler`

### Version info:

```

Commit 8f5b7ca12ad (2024-10-16 10:53 UTC)

  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:

  LLVM: libLLVM-16.0.6 (ORCJIT, znver2)
Threads: 16 default, 0 interactive, 8 GC (on 16 virtual cores)

  JULIA_DEPOT_PATH = /home/<my-user>/.cache/julia

Behaviour

When loading some packages in REPL, `REPLExt` throws an exception. 

### Expected behaviour

No exception thrown?

### Reproduce

To reproduce:
1. `julia-release --startup-file=no -t 1`
2. `using IJulia` (or other packages)

Packages that reproduce the bug:
- `IJulia`
- `Pluto`
- `CairoMakie`

Packages that don't reproduce the bug:
- `StaticArrays`
- `PackageCompiler`
